title: The Black Moth
description: The Black Moth — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Black Moth" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Epilogue"
memes.description: "His Grace of Andover sat at the window of his lodgings at Venice, looking down at a letter in his hand. The writing was his sister’s."

memes.label: "Prologue"
memes.description: "Clad in his customary black and silver, with raven hair unpowdered and elaborately dressed, diamonds on his fingers and in his cravat, Hugh Tracy Clare Belmanoir, Duke of Andover, sat at the escritoir."

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Andover Court
memes.description: The Duke of Andover's country estate in Sussex, where Diana Beauleigh is held captive by Tracy Belmanoir in the novel's climactic chapters.

memes.label: Andover Estate
memes.description: The ducal seat of the Belmanoir family — a cold, magnificent estate whose library, grounds, and reputation embody the pride, dangerous glamour, and moral vacancy of its master, the Duke of Andover.

memes.label: Andrew Belmanoir
memes.description: Lord Andrew Belmanoir, younger brother of the Duke of Andover — a "mad, reckless rakehell" who shares the Belmanoir wildness without Tracy's cold intelligence or magnetic control.

memes.label: Barn Elms
memes.description: The notorious duelling ground near London where Tracy Belmanoir fought his duel — a real historical location that anchors the novel's duelling culture in Georgian reality.

memes.label: Bath
memes.description: The fashionable Georgian spa city where much of the novel's social action takes place — a setting of balls, routs, and romantic encounters in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Bath Assembly Rooms and Theatre
memes.description: The Assembly Rooms in Bath, a centre of Georgian social entertainment where Diana Beauleigh encounters Devil Belmanoir in Chapter 7.

memes.label: Beau Nash
memes.description: Richard "Beau" Nash (1674–1761), the celebrated master of ceremonies at Bath, referenced in the novel as the arbiter of fashion and etiquette whose strict rules governed Georgian polite society.

memes.label: Beauleigh Residence
memes.description: The comfortable country home of the Beauleigh family — the domestic sanctuary where John Carstares recovers from his wounds and where his relationship with Diana deepens.

memes.label: The Belmanoir Family
memes.description: The ducal house of Belmanoir — the family whose pride, charm, and cold self-regard represent the dark side of aristocratic privilege in The Black Moth, and whose three siblings embody the spectrum of moral danger from reckless impulse to calculated cruelty.

memes.label: Carstares-Belmanoir Feud
memes.description: A cross-character timeline of the rivalry between the Carstares and Belmanoir families — the central interpersonal conflict that powers The Black Moth from the card-party scandal to the final duel.

memes.label: The Carstares Household
memes.description: The family establishment of the Earls of Wyncham — the household that Jack Carstares was exiled from and that Richard Carstares inherited, representing the novel's ideal of honourable aristocratic family life, tested by the cheating scandal and restored at the novel's end.

memes.label: "I: At the Chequers Inn, Fallowfield"
memes.description: "Chadber was the name of the host, florid of countenance, portly of person, and of manner pompous and urbane."

memes.label: "II: My Lord at the White Hart"
memes.description: "“Sir Anthony Ferndale” sat before the dressing-table in his room at the White Hart, idly polishing his nails."

memes.label: "III: Introducing the Hon. Richard Carstares"
memes.description: "Wyncham! A stately old house with mullioned windows, standing high on its stone terraces, half-covered by creepers; a house surrounded by lawns, rolling down on the one side to a river that rippled an."

memes.label: "IV: Introducing the Lady Lavinia Carstares"
memes.description: "Richard went slowly back to his chair. After a moment he sat down, staring blankly out of the window, his hands loosely clasped on the desk before him. So he remained for a long while, immobile."

memes.label: "V: His Grace of Andover"
memes.description: "Lady Lavinia dressed herself with even more than her usual care next afternoon, and well-nigh drove her maid distracted by her flashes of temper and impatient, contradictory orders."

memes.label: "VI: Bath: 29 Queen Square"
memes.description: "The autumn and the winter passed smoothly, and April found the Carstares installed at Bath, whither Lady Lavinia had teased her husband into going, despite his desire to return to Wyncham and John."

memes.label: "VII: Introducing Sundry New Characters"
memes.description: "Not twenty minutes’ walk from Lady Lavinia’s house in Queen Square resided a certain Madam Thompson—a widow—who had lived in Bath for nearly fifteen years."

memes.label: "VIII: The Biter Bit"
memes.description: "With John Carstares the winter had passed quite uneventfully."

memes.label: "IX: Lady O’Hara Intervenes"
memes.description: "Lady O’Hara found that her big, indolent husband was unusually silent next morning at breakfast."

memes.label: "X: Lady O’Hara Retires"
memes.description: "For a long minute silence reigned, all three actors in the little comedy listening to the heavy footsteps retreating down the passage, Carstares with one arm still around my lady’s waist and a rather ."

memes.label: "XI: My Lord Turns Rescuer and Comes Nigh Ending His Life"
memes.description: "Late that afternoon Carstares left Thurze House on one of his friend’s horses."

memes.label: "XII: My Lord Dictates a Letter and Receives a Visitor"
memes.description: "My lord came sighing back to life. He opened his eyes wearily, and turned his head. A faint feeling of surprise stole over him."

memes.label: "XIII: My Lord Makes His Bow"
memes.description: "After Jim’s arrival my lord recovered quickly, each day making great progress, much to the doctor’s satisfaction, who never tired of telling Mr."

memes.label: "XIV: Mistress Diana Is Unmaidenly"
memes.description: "The idyllic summer days passed quickly by, and every time that my lord spoke of leaving, the outcry was so indignant and so firm that he hastily subsided and told himself he would stay just another fe."

memes.label: "XV: O’Hara’s Mind Is Made Up"
memes.description: "Jim Salter folded one of my lord’s waistcoats, and placed it carefully in an open valise; then he picked up a coat, and spread it on the bed preparatory to folding it in such wise that no crease shoul."

memes.label: "XVI: Mr. Bettison Proposes"
memes.description: "Mr. Bettison could make nothing of Diana of late. Her demeanour, at first so charming and so cheerful, had become listless, and even chilling."

memes.label: "XVII: Lady O’Hara Wins Her Point"
memes.description: "Lady O’Hara looked across at her sleeping husband with no little severity in her glance. He was stretched in a chair beneath a giant oak, and she was busied with some needlework a few paces from him."

memes.label: "XVIII: Enter Captain Harold Lovelace"
memes.description: "At the end of August, after having spent a moderately quiet summer in the country, Lady Lavinia was again seized with a longing for town and its attractions."

memes.label: "XIX: The Reappearance of His Grace of Andover"
memes.description: "It seemed to Richard in the days that followed, that Captain Lovelace was never out of his house."

memes.label: "XX: His Grace of Andover Takes a Hand in the Game"
memes.description: "When the Duke of Andover dined next day at Grosvenor Square, he contrived, by subtle means, to make his sister feel inexplicably ill at ease."

memes.label: "XXI: Mrs. Fanshawe Lights a Fire and O’Hara Fans the Flame"
memes.description: "Richard Carstares very soon availed himself of Mrs. Fanshawe’s permission to call upon her, and duly put in an appearance at No. 16, Mount Street."

memes.label: "XXII: Developments"
memes.description: "After the encounter with O’Hara, whatever peace of mind Richard had had, left him."

memes.label: "XXIII: Lady Lavinia Goes to the Play"
memes.description: "Richard was away from home all next day, and his wife had plenty of time in which to meditate upon her situation."

memes.label: "XXIV: Richard Plays the Man"
memes.description: "Lady Lavinia’s frame of mind when she awoke next morning was hardly befitting one who contemplated an elopement. A weight seemed to rest on her chest, hopeless misery was gathered about her head."

memes.label: "XXV: His Grace of Andover Captures the Queen"
memes.description: "Diana stood in the old oak porch, riding-whip in hand, and the folds of her voluminous gown over her arm. Miss Betty stood beside her, surveying her with secret pride."

memes.label: "XXVI: My Lord Rides to Frustrate His Grace"
memes.description: "My lord yawned most prodigiously and let fall the Spectator. His eyes roved towards the clock, and noted with disgust that the hands pointed to half after five. He sighed and picked up the Rambler."

memes.label: "XXVII: My Lord Enters by the Window"
memes.description: "His Grace of Andover made a sign to the footmen, and with a sinking heart Diana watched them leave the room, discreetly closing the door behind them."

memes.label: "XXVIII: In Which What Threatened to Be Tragedy Turns to Comedy"
memes.description: "With a smothered cry Diana flew across the room to where my lord lay in a pitiful little heap, but before her was Richard. He fell on his knees beside the still figure, feeling for the wound."

memes.label: "XXIX: Lady O’Hara Is Triumphant"
memes.description: "After spending a restless night, starting at every sound, and hearing the hours strike slowly away, Lady O’Hara arose not a whit refreshed and considerably more ill at ease than she had been before."

memes.label: "XXX: Epilogue"
memes.description: "Redirect to Epilogue."

memes.label: Mr. Chadber
memes.description: The florid, pompous, and staunchly Tory landlord of the Chequers Inn at Fallowfield — the novel's opening scene-setter and a vivid portrait of provincial English character.

memes.label: Characters of The Black Moth
memes.description: A dynamic index of all characters in The Black Moth, grouped by role and family affiliation — rendered automatically from page frontmatter.

memes.label: Charles Holt
memes.description: A young kinsman of the Fleming family, fashionably dressed in apple-green and pink, who attends the theatre with the Carstares party.

memes.label: The Chequers Inn
memes.description: The inn at Fallowfield where the novel opens — a Tory stronghold, the stage for Jack Carstares's first appearance, and a vivid portrait of provincial English life in the 1750s.

memes.label: The Fallowfield Road
memes.description: The stretch of road near Fallowfield where John Carstares plies his trade as a gentleman highwayman — the novel's primary setting for encounters between the disguised aristocrat and the travellers he robs.

memes.label: Jack Cholmondely
memes.description: A London gentleman and duellist who fought a duel against Lord Falmouth in Bath's Crescent Fields, part of the novel's duelling subculture.

memes.label: Social Class and Rank
memes.description: The rigid yet permeable hierarchy of Georgian society — from duke to innkeeper — that structures every interaction in The Black Moth and defines the stakes of its central conflicts.

memes.label: Coach Travel and the Road
memes.description: The coaching system — mail coaches, private carriages, post-chaises, and the great roads of England — that connected Georgian society and provided the setting for chance encounters, abductions, and escapes in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Colonel Lord Robert Belmanoir
memes.description: Lavinia Carstares's brother, a colonel in appearance and manner — richly clad, rakish, and a member of the Belmanoir family whose casual chaperonage Lavinia prefers to Richard's solicitous care.

memes.label: Colonel Shepherd
memes.description: A colonel living on the Strand whom the Duke of Andover consults, leaving the meeting with evident satisfaction.

memes.label: Courage and Recklessness
memes.description: The intertwined qualities of physical bravery and heedless daring that defined the Georgian aristocratic male ideal — courage as the gentleman's cardinal virtue, and its shadow, recklessness, as his characteristic vice.

memes.label: Crescent Fields, Bath
memes.description: The duelling ground near Bath where Cholmondely and Falmouth fought their duel — one of the novel's key locations for the culture of honour and violence.

memes.label: Crossdown Woods
memes.description: The wooded area near the Beauleigh residence where the novel's climactic action unfolds — a liminal space between the domestic world and the dangers of the road.

memes.label: Debt and Credit
memes.description: The perpetual state of financial obligation that defined Georgian gentlemanly life — debts of honour, gaming debts, and tradesmen's bills — and a major driver of plot and character in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Diana Beauleigh
memes.description: The novel's heroine — a young woman of quiet dignity and "indomitable" loyalty, whose love for the disgraced John Carstares drives the romantic plot of The Black Moth to its triumphant conclusion.

memes.label: Disgrace and Exile
memes.description: The social condition of being cast out from one's family and class through a perceived moral failing — the central predicament of the novel's protagonist and a recurring thematic concern throughout The Black Moth.

memes.label: Duelling
memes.description: The practice of settling disputes of honour through formal armed combat, a defining social institution of Georgian-era gentleman culture and a recurring plot device in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Elopement and Clandestine Marriage
memes.description: The practice of marrying without parental consent — by flight, abduction, or clandestine ceremony — as a central plot engine and moral touchstone in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Fashion and Appearance
memes.description: The elaborate system of dress, grooming, and self-presentation through which Georgian society read rank, character, and moral worth — a silent language spoken fluently by every character in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Fittering
memes.description: A village near the Carstares estate in Sussex, home to Jim Salter's sweetheart Mary and the site of a horse-fair where Salter purchased his mare Jenny.

memes.label: Frank Fortescue
memes.description: The Honourable Frank Fortescue, friend and occasional companion to the Duke of Andover — a gentleman of the rakish set whose presence illuminates the social world of gaming and danger that Tracy Belmanoir dominates.

memes.label: Gaming and Gambling
memes.description: The gentleman's vice of wagering money at cards, dice, and other games — a central pastime and source of ruin in Georgian high society and a recurring motif in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Gentleman's Code of Conduct
memes.description: The unwritten yet rigorously enforced behavioural code governing dress, speech, honour, and conduct that defined the Georgian gentleman — the social framework within which all characters in The Black Moth operate.

memes.label: Gregory Markham
memes.description: A heavily handsome baronet and gambler, one of Lavinia Carstares's circle of admirers and a fixture at Lord Avon's card table.

memes.label: Harold Lovelace
memes.description: A handsome and charming captain, former flame of Lady Lavinia Carstares, whose re-entry into society threatens the Carstares' marriage and draws the attention of the Duke of Andover.

memes.label: Harper
memes.description: Servant in the household of Tracy Belmanoir, Duke of Andover, who later enters the service of Diana Beauleigh under the assumed identity of Sir Hugh Grandison.

memes.label: Highway Robbery
memes.description: The practice of armed robbery on public highways, which in Georgian England occupied an ambiguous position between common crime and the romanticised exploits of the "gentleman highwayman" — a central narrative device in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: The unwritten code governing a gentleman's conduct, reputation, and social standing — the moral backbone of Georgian society and the central value system against which every character in The Black Moth is measured and found wanting.

memes.label: Honour System in The Black Moth
memes.description: Redirect to Honour System in The Black Moth — the primary cross-concept synthesis page for the Georgian honour system.

memes.label: Honour System in The Black Moth
memes.description: A cross-concept synthesis of the Georgian honour system as depicted in The Black Moth — tracking the interlocking codes of gentlemanly conduct, duelling, social reputation, and disgrace that drive the novel's central conflicts.

memes.label: Horton House
memes.description: The Sussex home of the Beauleigh family — Diana, Miss Betty, and Mr. Beauleigh — and the setting where the romance between John Carstares and Diana Beauleigh quietly deepens. (012-074)

memes.label: Isabella Fanshawe
memes.description: A sprightly and beautiful widow, met by John Carstares in Vienna, who later becomes an unwitting catalyst in the marital crisis between Richard and Lavinia Carstares.

memes.label: The Jacobite Cause
memes.description: The political movement to restore the Stuart dynasty to the British throne — the cause that shaped the political loyalties of the novel's characters and haunts the background of The Black Moth.

memes.label: Jim Davenant
memes.description: A former friend of John Carstares who, like others, abandoned him after the cheating scandal — later among those bidden to the reconciliation dinner at the novel's end.

memes.label: Jim Salter
memes.description: John Carstares's loyal servant and partner in his highwayman career — the faithful "Jim" who has been at Jack's side throughout six years of exile, managing the practical side of the gentleman highwayman's trade with gruff devotion and unwavering fidelity.

memes.label: John Carstares
memes.description: The disgraced eldest son of the Earl of Wyncham, who assumes the identity of a gentleman highwayman after taking the blame for his brother's cheating at cards — the protagonist of The Black Moth.

memes.label: Sir John Fortescue
memes.description: Sir John Fortescue, an austere patrician and father of Frank Fortescue — a figure of the older generation who moves through the novel's social scenes with gravity and reserve.

memes.label: Lady Carlyle
memes.description: A London society lady, described as Lavinia Carstares's "pet rival in all matters of fashion."

memes.label: Lady Charlwood
memes.description: A lady mistaken for Diana Beauleigh by the Duke of Andover during a masquerade ball.

memes.label: Lady Elizabeth Beauleigh
memes.description: Lady Elizabeth Beauleigh, the deceased mother of Diana Beauleigh — mentioned only in passing, her absence frames Diana's position as an orphaned young woman under the guardianship of her aunt.

memes.label: Lady Fanny
memes.description: A minor character in The Black Moth, a lady who assists Lavinia Carstares with her toilette before a social event.

memes.label: Lady Gunning
memes.description: Miss Gunning — later Lady Gunning — one of the celebrated "beautiful Gunnings" of Bath society, a fashionable beauty whose marriage to Coventry is the talk of the town.

memes.label: Lavinia Carstares
memes.description: Lady Lavinia Carstares — born a Belmanoir, married to Richard Carstares, sister to the Duke of Andover — the woman whose beauty and charm made her the unwitting cause of the novel's central tragedy, and whose emotional journey from self-absorption to loyalty mirrors the novel's moral arc.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The great metropolis that forms the distant backdrop to The Black Moth — the centre of power, fashion, and danger from which the novel's characters have come and to which some may return.

memes.label: The London Social Season
memes.description: The annual rhythm of Georgian high society — a glittering round of routs, balls, assemblies, and masquerades — that provides the social backdrop for courtship, intrigue, and scandal in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Lord Avon
memes.description: An earl and generous host whose card-room at Catharine Place is a gathering place for the novel's gentleman gamblers, known for his loud laugh and convivial nature.

memes.label: Lord Falmouth
memes.description: A London gentleman and friend of Frank Fortescue, who served as the Duke of Andover's second in a duel against Fortescue in Hyde Park.

memes.label: Lord Fotheringham
memes.description: A minor character in The Black Moth, a gentleman at a social gathering who is promised a dance with Lavinia Carstares.

memes.label: Lord March
memes.description: A minor character in The Black Moth, a gentleman who encounters Lavinia Carstares in the foyer of a social venue.

memes.label: Lord Robert Belmanoir
memes.description: Younger brother of the Duke of Andover, a colonel with a military bearing who shares the Belmanoir family traits of pride and coldness, though in a milder form than his brother Tracy.

memes.label: Madam Thompson
memes.description: A widow living in Queen Square, Bath, who hosts Diana Beauleigh and her aunt — her introduction of the "simple Mr. Everard" (Tracy Belmanoir) to Diana sets a key plot in motion.

memes.label: Marriage and Matrimony
memes.description: The institution of marriage as both a social contract and a romantic ideal in Georgian England — the destination towards which the novel's plot drives, and the lens through which Heyer examines questions of love, money, consent, and class in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Masquerade Balls and Disguise
memes.description: A Georgian social institution allowing gentlemen and ladies to dance, converse, and intrigue anonymously — and, in The Black Moth, the masquerade that enables a fatal case of mistaken identity, setting the abduction plot in motion.

memes.label: Miles O'Hara
memes.description: An Irish gentleman and John Carstares's closest friend — the first person to recognise Jack beneath his highwayman's disguise and the novel's model of warm, loyal, companionate friendship.

memes.label: Miss Betty Beauleigh
memes.description: Diana Beauleigh's aunt and chaperone — a warm, shrewd, and protective presence whose quiet support of Diana's unconventional choices provides the moral backbone of the novel's domestic scenes.

memes.label: Molly O'Hara
memes.description: Lady Molly O'Hara, the charming Irish wife of Miles and warm-hearted friend to Diana Beauleigh — a model of companionate marriage and spirited femininity in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Mr. Beauleigh
memes.description: Diana Beauleigh's kindly but conventional father — a gentleman of modest means whose secret hope for a respectable match yields to genuine joy when his daughter finds love with the Earl of Wyncham.

memes.label: Mr. Bettison
memes.description: A respectable but uninspiring suitor for Diana Beauleigh's hand — the man she refuses because she will not marry without love, and whose rejection defines her commitment to romantic principle over social convenience.

memes.label: Mr. Chilter
memes.description: The timid and long-suffering clerk to Mr. Fudby, a city merchant, who accompanies his employer to Lewes after a highway robbery.

memes.label: Mr. Devereux
memes.description: Mr. Devereux, a gentleman of Bath society who appears at the theatre alongside Markham and Wilding — a minor figure in the social world of the novel, possibly connected to Lady Devereux.

memes.label: Mr. Fudby
memes.description: A pompous and self-important city merchant who becomes a victim of John Carstares's highway robbery and later embellishes the tale to his own glorification.

memes.label: Mr. Hedges
memes.description: The fussy and dyspeptic mayor of Lewes, summoned from his dinner to hear Mr. Fudby's embellished account of a highway robbery.

memes.label: Mr. Selwyn
memes.description: A society gentleman, gambler, and one-time admirer of Lavinia Carstares, known for his wit and his notorious love of horrors.

memes.label: Mr. Standish
memes.description: A minor character in The Black Moth, described as a "bowing acquaintance" who arrives at a social gathering.

memes.label: Mr. Stapely
memes.description: A minor character in The Black Moth, a gentleman who serves as a dance partner and identifies Lavinia Carstares to another guest.

memes.label: Mr. Walpole
memes.description: Historical figure Horace Walpole (1717–1797), author and antiquarian, who appears briefly as a London acquaintance of the Duke of Andover and host of a card-party at Twickenham.

memes.label: Mr. Welsh
memes.description: A young gentleman who fought a duel with John Carstares ("Master Jack") in a spinney some years before the events of the novel; remembered fondly by the local folk as evidence of Jack's swordsmanship and chivalry.

memes.label: Mrs. Clive
memes.description: A celebrated historical actress (Kitty Clive, 1711–1785) referenced in the novel's Bath theatre scenes — part of the Georgian theatrical world that forms the backdrop to Lavinia's social theatre-going.

memes.label: Mrs. Fanshawe
memes.description: A lady of Bath society who becomes a confidante and supporter of Jack Carstares (Sir Anthony Ferndale), hearing his confession and championing his cause.

memes.label: O'Hara Residence
memes.description: The warm Irish household of Miles and Molly O'Hara — a setting of friendship, comedy, and companionate marriage that provides the novel's most attractive model of domestic happiness.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The French capital mentioned in the novel's backstory — the place of exile, intrigue, and Continental glamour that haunts the edges of the novel's English world.

memes.label: Places of The Black Moth
memes.description: A geographic index of all locations in The Black Moth — from the great estates of Wyncham and Andover to the coaching inns, spa towns, and London streets that form the novel's physical world.

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: The fierce sense of personal and familial dignity that governed the behaviour of Georgian aristocrats — a double-edged virtue that could sustain honour through adversity or harden into the destructive arrogance embodied by the Belmanoir family in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Queen Square
memes.description: "Redirect to Queen Square, Bath."

memes.label: Queen Square, Bath
memes.description: The fashionable address in Bath where Lady Lavinia Carstares stays — a centre of social manoeuvring and domestic drama in the novel.

memes.label: Ranelagh Gardens
memes.description: The fashionable Ranelagh Pleasure Gardens in Chelsea, London — a Georgian amusement venue where Lavinia Carstares and Tracy Belmanoir cross paths in a telling social encounter.

memes.label: Richard Carstares
memes.description: The younger brother of John Carstares, who inherited the earldom after his brother's disgrace — a man haunted by the secret that his brother took the blame for a crime Richard himself committed.

memes.label: Scandal and Reputation
memes.description: The social mechanism by which Georgian society policed its members — the whispered ruin that could destroy a reputation overnight, and the narrative engine driving The Black Moth's central plot.

memes.label: Servants and Service
memes.description: The world of domestic service that sustained Georgian aristocratic life — the footmen, maids, valets, and innkeepers who populate the margins of The Black Moth and whose loyalty, insight, and occasional comedy provide the novel's ground-level view of its social world.

memes.label: Sir Denis Fabian
memes.description: A baronet mentioned dismissively as "that great gaby" by Lavinia Carstares, whom her brother Richard has been inviting to the house as a potential suitor.

memes.label: Sir Douglas Faversham
memes.description: A London gentleman who, with Sir Gregory Markham and others, calls upon Lavinia Carstares at her London residence.

memes.label: Sir Hugh Grandison
memes.description: A fictitious identity used by the Duke of Andover at White's Chocolate House — the name under which he provides false references for the groom Harper, enabling his scheme against Diana Beauleigh.

memes.label: St. James's, London
memes.description: The aristocratic district of London where the novel's fashionable world centres — synonymous with the Court, the Season, and the highest reaches of Georgian society.

memes.label: Sussex
memes.description: The southern English county that forms the principal rural setting of the novel — home to Diana Beauleigh, the hunting-ground of the highwayman Carstares, and the landscape against which the story's central romance unfolds. (006-038)

memes.label: The Black Moth
memes.description: Overview and navigation hub for Georgette Heyer's 1921 novel The Black Moth — a Georgian-era romance and adventure story of honour, disgrace, and redemption.

memes.label: The Card Party
memes.description: The fateful card party six years before the novel opens where Richard Carstares cheated and John Carstares took the blame — the foundational event that sets the entire plot in motion and the moral wound at the heart of the Carstares family.

memes.label: The Climactic Confrontation
memes.description: The climactic confrontation between John Carstares and Tracy Belmanoir — the novel's dramatic peak where courage meets coldness and the moral forces of the story collide.

memes.label: The 'Forty-Five Rising
memes.description: The Jacobite rising of 1745 — the historical event that forms the political backdrop to The Black Moth, shaping the loyalties and identities of the novel's characters.

memes.label: The Highway Rescue
memes.description: The dramatic roadside rescue of Diana Beauleigh from highwaymen by John Carstares — the event that launches the novel's romantic plot and establishes the hero's courage.

memes.label: The Masquerade Ball
memes.description: A masked ball hosted by Lady Carlyle where Tracy Belmanoir attempts to approach Lavinia Carstares in disguise, and Harold Lovelace solidifies his position in Lavinia's social orbit.

memes.label: The Pump Room
memes.description: The historic Pump Room in Bath, a centre of Georgian social life where visitors drink the spa waters and promenade — the setting of the novel's opening social scenes.

memes.label: The Queensberry Rout
memes.description: The fashionable gathering — a rout or masquerade — where the novel's characters converge in a scene of social manoeuvring, danger, and romantic tension.

memes.label: Thurze House
memes.description: The Maltby residence of Sir Miles and Lady Molly O'Hara — a place of Irish warmth and hospitality where John Carstares finds safe harbour after leaving Horton House. (011-001)

memes.label: Tom Wilding
memes.description: A gentleman about town and friend of Gregory Markham, part of the fashionable London set — present at social gatherings and gaming scenes throughout the novel.

memes.label: Tory Politics
memes.description: The Tory-Whig political divide in Georgian England, centred on Jacobite loyalty versus Hanoverian allegiance, which provides the historical texture and familial identity markers in The Black Moth.

memes.label: Tracy Belmanoir
memes.description: Hugh Tracy Clare Belmanoir, Duke of Andover — the rakish, magnetic antagonist of The Black Moth, whose cold charm and complete lack of moral scruple make him the dark counterpoint to the novel's hero.

memes.label: Warburton
memes.description: The Carstares family solicitor — a shrewd, loyal lawyer sent by Richard to find his exiled brother and persuade him to return to Wyncham.

memes.label: The White Hart
memes.description: The inn where 'Sir Anthony Ferndale' prepares for his highwayman exploits — the setting of some of the novel's most intimate scenes of John Carstares in disguise.

memes.label: White's Chocolate House
memes.description: The historic chocolate house on St. James's Street, London — a fashionable resort for gentlemen of quality in the early 18th century, mentioned in connection with Sir Hugh Grandison.

memes.label: Wigs and Grooming in Georgian Society
memes.description: The art of wig-wearing and male grooming as a theatre of status, character, and disguise in Georgian England — a ritual central to the gentleman's public identity in The Black Moth.

memes.label: William Fleming
memes.description: A short, tubby country gentleman of Scottish farming stock, married to a cousin of the Belmanoir family — a mésalliance the family has never quite forgiven.

memes.label: Wyncham
memes.description: The ancestral seat of the Carstares family — an old Tory stronghold from which John Carstares was exiled and to which his honourable return constitutes the novel's emotional and moral resolution.

memes.label: Wyncham House
memes.description: The Carstares family's London townhouse in Mayfair, refurnished in the French Court style by Lavinia, serving as the social hub for the Earl of Wyncham and his circle.
